evidence_excerpt: A diagnosis of probable CJD was made … She died 1 year after the onset of clinical signs.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: During the days after admission she presented with nocturnal confusion and agitation. The ataxia increased… choreatiform-athetotic movements… She became tetraspastic.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: During the days after admission she presented with nocturnal confusion and agitation. The ataxia increased.
She became tetraspastic and more and more aspontaneous.
Percutaneous endoscopic gastrostomy (PEG) for feeding became necessary.
She died 1 year after the onset of clinical signs.

notes_on_uncertainty: Exact diagnosis date is not specified; admission when probable CJD was diagnosed was used as the index time. Death occurred about 11 months after diagnosis (1 year after onset), mapped to mid_term. Timing of specific deteriorations and PEG placement was described as occurring over days after admission, mapped to short_term.
